note_data.title: Business documents
keyPoints: A **business document** is a written record that provides evidence of a business transaction
An **invoice** is sent by the seller to the buyer showing goods sold, quantity, price and amount due
A **receipt** is given by the seller to the buyer as proof that payment has been received
A **credit note** is sent by the seller to the buyer to reduce the amount owed when goods are returned or overcharged
Business documents provide **audit trail** for checking accuracy of accounting records
Documents must contain **essential information** including date, names, amounts and transaction details
**Debit note** is sent by the buyer to the seller requesting a credit note for returns or overcharges
**Statement of account** shows the balance owed by a customer at a particular date
Documents help resolve **disputes** between buyer and seller about transactions

definitions.definition: A document sent by the seller to the buyer showing details of goods sold and amount due for payment
A document given by the seller to the buyer as proof that payment has been received
A document sent by the seller to the buyer to reduce the amount owed when goods are returned or an overcharge has occurred
A written record that provides evidence of a business transaction

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Name the document or pick out the relevant information from the question

modelAnswer.question: Explain why a credit note would be issued by Karachi Traders to its customer. (3 marks)
modelAnswer.answer: A credit note would be issued when goods are returned by the customer because they are faulty or unwanted. It would also be issued if there has been an overcharge on the original invoice because the wrong price was used. The credit note reduces the amount owed by the customer because it corrects errors or adjusts for returned merchandise.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Reduces amount owed by customer

commonMistakes: Never confuse invoice with receipt — invoice requests payment, receipt confirms payment received
Students often think credit note increases amount owed — it always reduces the debt
Don't say 'bill' in exams — always use the term 'invoice' for formal business documents
Never say credit note is sent by buyer — it is always sent by the seller to the buyer
Students mix up debit note and credit note — debit note is sent BY buyer TO seller
examinerTips: Business documents appear in every Paper 1 and Paper 2 exam
Always state WHO sends the document TO whom for full marks
Learn the direction of flow: Invoice seller→buyer, Receipt seller→buyer, Credit note seller→buyer
Questions often ask for TWO reasons why documents are issued — prepare multiple examples
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Flow diagram showing seller and buyer with arrows indicating direction of documents: Invoice (seller to buyer), Payment (buyer to seller), Receipt (seller to buyer), Credit note (seller to buyer for returns)
